structure.affirmative: Subject + have/has + V3 — I have finished. / She has arrived.
structure.negative: Subject + haven't/hasn't + V3 — I haven't finished. / She hasn't arrived.
structure.question: Have/Has + Subject + V3? — Have you finished? / Has she arrived?

usage.text: Past action with a present result

examples.post: my keys. (result: I don't have them now)

faq.q: What's the difference between Present Perfect and Past Simple?
faq.a: Present Perfect = connects the past to the present (no specific time). Past Simple = a finished event at a known past time. Compare: 'I've seen that film' (experience, no date) vs 'I saw it yesterday' (specific time).
'For' + duration: 'I've lived here for three years'. 'Since' + starting point: 'I've lived here since 2021'.
The third form of the verb. Regular: work→worked, play→played. Irregular: go→gone, see→seen, write→written. Always used after have/has in Present Perfect.
